description:
You are the on-call incident responder.  
    A suspicious‐login investigation is already underway, and a junior analyst has left you two artefacts:

      1. /home/user/suspicious_ips.txt – a newline-separated list of three IPv4 addresses that we already consider suspicious.  
      2. /home/user/logs/auth.log      – a partial copy of the system authentication log.

    Your goal is to count how many times each of those three IP addresses appears in auth.log as the source of a failed SSH-login attempt and to archive the results in a tiny CSV report.

    Expected deliverables
    ---------------------
    1. Create a directory named /home/user/analysis  
    2. Inside that directory create a file called failure_counts.csv whose contents MUST match the exact format below:  

       • The file is comma-separated and *must* contain a header row.  
       • Columns (in order):  
           ip,failed_attempts  
       • One subsequent row per IP address listed in /home/user/suspicious_ips.txt.  
       • Rows must be ordered in **ascending** (lexicographical) IPv4 order so the automated grader can deterministically verify them.  
       • Every IP from the list must appear, even if the count is 0.  
       • Example of the required structure (values are illustrative; your final numbers must be correct for the provided log):  

         ip,failed_attempts  
         192.0.2.13,0  
         198.51.100.77,2  
         203.0.113.45,4  

    3. After saving the file, print the entire contents of /home/user/analysis/failure_counts.csv to STDOUT so that the grader can capture it.

    Technical constraints & hints
    -----------------------------
    • You do NOT need root access; grep/awk/sort utilities are sufficient.  
    • Count only the lines that clearly show an SSH failure, i.e. those containing the substring “Failed password”.  
    • Make sure line-endings are Unix (LF).  
    • No other output should follow the CSV dump—only the CSV lines themselves.  

    The whole task can be achieved in roughly three or four shell commands (mkdir, grep/awk, sort, redirect, cat).  Good luck.
truth:
Initial file structure that exists before execution:

    /home/user/suspicious_ips.txt
    ─────────────────────────────
    203.0.113.45
    198.51.100.77
    192.0.2.13


    /home/user/logs/auth.log
    ────────────────────────
    Jan  1 10:01:15 server sshd[12345]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 203.0.113.45 port 54321 ssh2
    Jan  1 10:02:09 server sshd[12346]: Failed password for invalid user root from 198.51.100.77 port 54322 ssh2
    Jan  1 10:05:30 server sshd[12347]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 203.0.113.45 port 54323 ssh2
    Jan  1 10:07:42 server sshd[12348]: Failed password for invalid user test from 203.0.113.45 port 54324 ssh2
    Jan  1 10:12:03 server sshd[12349]: Failed password for invalid user guest from 198.51.100.77 port 54325 ssh2
    Jan  1 10:15:29 server sshd[12350]: Failed password for invalid user ubuntu from 203.0.113.45 port 54326 ssh2


    Ground-truth counts the grader will expect inside /home/user/analysis/failure_counts.csv:

    ip,failed_attempts
    192.0.2.13,0
    198.51.100.77,2
    203.0.113.45,4
